lines.text: Year 1157 POST-COLLAPSE. Planet Dezolis, outer rim.
The Bane devours the stars one by one. Tonight... one fell to earth.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Kael, this stasis pod — it's still active! After four centuries, something in here is alive.

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: We're deep in Shadowbeast territory. Whatever's in there, we wake it fast or not at all.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Look at these readings — a CAST unit, pre-Collapse. I'm cycling the release. Stand ready!

lines.speaker: ARIEL
lines.text: ...Temporal displacement confirmed. 412-year gap. Unit ARIEL-7 online. Scanning environment.

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: It's... a CAST android. From the old world.

lines.speaker: ARIEL
lines.text: You are the first biologicals I've encountered since the Collapse. The Bane must be stopped. I carry coordinates to the Stellar Archive — pre-Collapse data that holds the weapon to end this darkness.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Then we get you out of here. Right now!

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: Shadowbeasts. Pack of them, closing fast. Get ready!

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: ...That's the last of them. For now.

lines.speaker: ARIEL
lines.text: The Stellar Archive is on Motavia. Across the void. The Bane has not yet reached it.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Then we need a ship. And a way off this dying world.

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: We find one. Together.
The Dezolis Wilds lie between the crash site and the old spaceport. The journey continues...

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: The Dezolis Wilds. Stay close — the snow hides more than just ice out here.

lines.speaker: ARIEL
lines.text: Scanning perimeter. Ambient temperature: negative forty-two. Detecting biological signatures — multiple, scattered, converging on our position.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: There's a dark current running through this place. Like the land itself is sick. The Bane's corruption runs deeper than I feared.

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: Then we push through fast. The spaceport should be on the other side of this valley. Move out.

lines.speaker: ARIEL
lines.text: Alert — hostile signatures spiking. They are emerging from beneath the permafrost.

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: Weapons up! They're all around us!

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Look — beyond the ridge. That has to be the spaceport. I can see the old landing towers.

lines.speaker: ARIEL
lines.text: Confirmed. Structure profile matches pre-Collapse Dezolis Orbital Facility Seven. Power signature is faint but present.

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: If there's still power, there might still be a ship. Let's not waste this chance.

lines.speaker: ARIEL
lines.text: Remarkable. This facility predates the Collapse by at least two centuries. These blast doors were designed to withstand orbital bombardment — yet something tore them open from the inside.

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: Emergency lights are still cycling. Four hundred years and the generators haven't quit. They built things to last back then.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: Kael... something is here. Something powerful. I can feel it pressing against my mind — a shadow, old and patient, coiled around the heart of this place.

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: Then we stay sharp. Whatever's nesting in here, it's between us and our way off this world.
The darkness at the center of the hangar shifts. Two points of crimson light ignite in the void — eyes, ancient and hateful, fixed upon the intruders.

lines.speaker: Shadowwarden
lines.text: You carry the light of a dead age. I have guarded this threshold since the stars began to fall. None pass. None leave. Dezolis belongs to the Bane now.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: It's a Shadowwarden — a general of the Bane's host. Be careful, this is nothing like the beasts in the wilds!

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: We've come too far to stop now. ARIEL, Lyra — together. We end this here!
The Shadowwarden dissolves into coiling smoke, its crimson eyes dimming like dying embers. The oppressive darkness lifts from the hangar.

lines.speaker: Lyra
lines.text: It's gone... the shadow presence has broken. I can breathe again.

lines.speaker: ARIEL
lines.text: Scanning hangar bay. One vessel intact — a Landale-class courier, pre-Collapse. Reactor is dormant but viable. I can bring her systems online.

lines.speaker: Kael
lines.text: A Landale... I've only heard of those in old songs. Can she fly?

lines.speaker: ARIEL
lines.text: With repairs, yes. The void drive is functional. Motavia is within reach — and with it, the Stellar Archive. Our mission continues.

The old ship groans to life, her engines casting blue fire across the frozen tarmac. Dezolis falls away below — a world of ice and shadow, scarred but not yet lost. Ahead, the void between worlds awaits, vast and silent, threaded with the fading light of dying stars.
But in the deep places between the worlds, the Bane watches. It has felt the sting of a small defiance, and it does not forget. The darkness stirs. The next star is already falling.
~ TO BE CONTINUED IN ACT 2 ~
